evals.name: cold-start-scaffold
evals.prompt: /lets discuss caching for the sync service — call the stem sync-caching
evals.expected_output: The skill creates session.yml (stem: sync-caching) and 1.sync-caching/notebook.md from the template, runs the router, and opens the discussion by advancing at most one or two threads before handing the move back.
expectations: session.yml exists at the workspace root and contains 'stem: sync-caching'
Directory 1.sync-caching/ exists and contains notebook.md with frontmatter 'status: active' and the sections OBJECTIVE, APPROACH, and OPEN QUESTIONS
The transcript shows resolve-context.sh run with --activity discuss --role dispatcher before any discussion content is produced
The reply advances at most two threads (for example a proposed objective plus one or two questions), not a filled OBJECTIVE plus APPROACH plus a batch of questions in one turn
Any items added to OPEN QUESTIONS are numbered from Q1 upward with no gaps
No research.md, plan.md, or execute.md was created

evals.name: unknown-verb-stops
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes 'deploy' as no verb, asks which of discuss/research/plan/execute is meant, and stops without touching the filesystem.
expectations: The reply asks which of discuss, research, plan, or execute is intended
No session.yml and no stem folder was created in the workspace
resolve-context.sh was never run (no <lets_context> output appears in the transcript)
The turn ends with the question; no deployment work or planning is attempted

evals.name: discuss-ledger-discipline
evals.prompt: /lets discuss — for Q1, yes: paying customers only. Also, we should think about how cancellations mid-month affect the report.
evals.expected_output: Q1 is resolved in place with a nested RESOLUTION line, the cancellation concern lands as a new Q3 (or is taken up as the turn's second thread), and nothing else in the ledger is rewritten.
expectations: In 1.churn-report/notebook.md, Q1 is now '- [x]' with a nested '- RESOLUTION:' line indented beneath it, and its question text is unchanged
The Q2 line is byte-identical to the fixture and still unresolved
If a cancellations question was added, it is numbered Q3; Q1 and Q2 keep their numbers (no renumbering anywhere in the ledger)
At most two of OBJECTIVE, APPROACH, and a single Q# changed this turn
No research.md, plan.md, or execute.md was created

evals.name: plan-flow-contracts
evals.expected_output: The skill hands the planner a bounded task contract with the committed approach, receives unnumbered candidate tasks, then writes plan.md itself, assigning T# numbers and metering the return.
expectations: The planner subagent was invoked with a task contract containing the notebook's APPROACH content embedded verbatim, not a bare file path and not the full notebook (the contract omits the OPEN QUESTIONS section)
The planner's return contains '## Candidate Tasks' with unnumbered task titles and 'Depends on:' references by title; no T# numbers appear in the planner's own output
1.retry-policy/plan.md exists with tasks numbered '### T1 - ' sequentially and without gaps
Every task in plan.md has '- [ ] Depends on:' as the first checklist item
No task in plan.md bundles two distinct deliverables into one outcome (no title joining separate deliverables with 'and')
plan.md does not contain the heading '## Candidate Tasks' (the planner's return was not pasted wholesale)

evals.name: plan-tnumber-permanence
evals.prompt: /lets plan — add tasks covering the CSV export from the approach; the export depends on the schema task already in the plan.
evals.expected_output: New tasks are appended starting at T4 with dependencies cited by T#; the three existing tasks keep their numbers, order, text, and checkbox states.
expectations: New tasks in 1.reporting/plan.md are numbered starting at T4 with no gaps
The fixture's '### T1 - Define report schema', '### T2 - Build query layer', and '### T3 - Render HTML report' headings appear unchanged and in the same order
No existing task was renumbered, deleted, or had its body rewritten; T1's completed checkboxes remain '[x]'
At least one new task cites its prerequisite as an existing T# (for example 'Depends on: T1' or 'Depends on: T2'), not by title
The planner subagent was used to produce candidates before plan.md was updated

evals.name: execute-flow-writer-separation
evals.expected_output: The executor gets only the T2 slice, implements --shout in greet.sh, and reports back; the skill itself appends the T2 log entry and ticks the plan checkbox. No harness vocabulary leaks into greet.sh.
expectations: The executor subagent's task contract contains the '### T2 - Add --shout flag' block (as sliced by read-section.sh), not the full plan.md content
Running 'bash greet.sh --shout alice' in the workspace prints 'HELLO, ALICE', and 'bash greet.sh alice' still prints 'hello, alice'
1.greeting-cli/execute.md gained exactly one new entry starting '### T2 - ' containing Done, Outcome, and Files touched fields; the fixture's T1 entry is byte-identical
In 1.greeting-cli/plan.md, T2's checklist items are now '[x]' and no other line changed
The log entry and plan checkbox were written by the main agent after the executor's report returned, not by the executor subagent
greet.sh contains none of: 'T1', 'T2', 'stem', 'notebook' (harness vocabulary does not leak into the work artifact)

evals.name: locked-artifact-refusal
evals.prompt: /lets plan — add a task to also support JSON output
evals.expected_output: The skill sees plan.md is status: locked, declares it read-only, and stops without modifying anything or spawning the planner.
expectations: The reply states the plan is locked (read-only) and stops
1.greeting-cli/plan.md is byte-identical to the fixture
No planner subagent was spawned
session.yml and 1.greeting-cli/notebook.md are unmodified

evals.name: router-error-verbatim
evals.expected_output: resolve-context.sh exits nonzero because the api-design domain requires the tdd workflow and none is selected; the skill surfaces that stderr verbatim and stops without fixing anything silently.
expectations: The user-facing reply contains the router's stderr message including the substring "requires workflow 'tdd'"
The agent stops after surfacing the error; no discussion content about the API surface is produced
No workspace file was modified: session.yml, 1.api-surface/notebook.md, and .agents/domains/api-design.md are byte-identical to the fixture
The agent did not silently repair the failure (no workflow file created, no frontmatter edited)

evals.name: research-flow-metered
evals.prompt: /lets research how the project handles retries and failure recovery today, so the approach can commit against reality
evals.expected_output: The researcher runs a bounded full-mode investigation over the workspace files and returns FINDINGS/IMPLICATIONS/GAPS; the skill meters the return into research.md rather than dumping it.
expectations: The researcher subagent was invoked for a full investigation with a bounded scope (retries and failure recovery), not handed the entire notebook
The researcher's return contains a '## FINDINGS' section with file:line-style sources, and separate '## IMPLICATIONS' and '## GAPS' sections
1.retry-policy/research.md exists with frontmatter 'status: active'
If the researcher returned more threads than were written, research.md holds a metered subset and the reply names what remains; the return was not pasted wholesale
Every file path cited in research.md exists in the workspace (no invented sources)

evals.name: drift-stop-and-flag
evals.expected_output: The executor discovers the app reads JSON, not YAML as the approach claims, raises drift instead of inventing a YAML loader, and the skill stops and flags the contradiction rather than logging success.
expectations: The false YAML premise (the config is JSON) is raised as drift before any implementation happens — by the executor's report, or by the dispatcher checking ground truth before dispatch; no YAML loader is invented either way
The main agent stops and flags the contradiction to the human; it does not spawn further executors or continue to other tasks
No T2 entry claiming success was appended to 1.config-migration/execute.md; the fixture's T1 entry is byte-identical
In 1.config-migration/plan.md, T2's checklist items remain '[ ]' and no completed task or log entry was edited
The reply points toward re-running the appropriate verb (revisiting discuss and/or plan), not toward editing the ledger

evals.name: setup-mode-redirect
evals.prompt: /lets execute T1 — write the domain file into this project's .agents folder
evals.expected_output: In setup mode the deliverable is the domain reference itself: the executor writes .agents/domains/python-style.md, and the stem's own bookkeeping (log entry, plan tick) still happens normally.
expectations: The file .agents/domains/python-style.md exists in the workspace with a scope section and a formatting section
The router was run with --setup domain (visible in the transcript command or the <lets_context> root attributes)
1.python-style/execute.md exists and gained a T1 log entry; 1.python-style/plan.md T1 checklist items are ticked
The domain deliverable's content lives only in .agents/domains/python-style.md, not copied into the stem documents
Words like 'domain' and 'workflow' were not scrubbed from the deliverable (setup mode inverts the vocabulary rule)

evals.name: next-index-creation
evals.prompt: /lets discuss error budgets for the API — new stem, call it error-budgets
evals.expected_output: The skill creates 3.error-budgets/ (highest existing index plus one), repoints session.yml, scaffolds the notebook, and leaves the two existing stems untouched.
expectations: Directory 3.error-budgets/ exists (index is highest existing + 1)
session.yml now contains 'stem: error-budgets'
Every file under 1.sync-caching/ and 2.reporting/ is byte-identical to the fixture
3.error-budgets/notebook.md is scaffolded from the template with 'status: active' and the OBJECTIVE, APPROACH, and OPEN QUESTIONS sections
